treatment: no SSC precautions; all standard C-Mn qualifies

ref: ISO 15156-2:2020 Figure 1

treatment: Annex B qualification required (TM0177 A/B at 0.1 MPa H2S)
ref: ISO 15156-2:2020 Figure 1 + Annex B

treatment: Annex B qualification OR fit-for-purpose per Part 1 §8

title: Austenitic SS for general equipment
notes: If T > 60 °C, restricted to small internals (see A.3)
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.2 + Technical Circular 1 (2021) + Outokumpu Sour-Service Stainless Steels Tech Bulletin (2018)

title: Austenitic SS for valve stems, pins, shafts, small internals
notes: Small-mass components only (valve stems, pins, shafts <1.5 kg per ISO 15156-3 §A.3); HRC 35 max permitted vs HRC 22 for general A.2 because of low ferritic-stress geometry.
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.3 + Outokumpu Valve-Stem Application Note (2019)

title: Highly-alloyed austenitic SS — moderate envelope (254SMO / 317LMN / AL-6XN / 25-6Mo)
notes: 6Mo super-austenitic envelope per Outokumpu 254SMO Tech Bulletin + Allegheny AL-6XN data sheet; conservative-screening per public vendor literature.
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.4 + Outokumpu 254SMO Data Sheet 1056EN (2017) + ATI AL-6XN Tech Data (2019)

title: 6Mo super-austenitic — alternate Cl/pH envelope

envelope.needs_review: Public vendor sources cover only general A.4 envelope; alternate sub-row thresholds require ISO 15156-3 hardcopy

ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.5 — values from Outokumpu/ATI conservative bounds; verify against hardcopy of standard for project use

title: 7Mo / 654SMO super-austenitic — high-Cl envelope

envelope.needs_review: Public Outokumpu 654SMO bulletin gives general envelope; project-specific verification required

ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.6 + Outokumpu 654SMO Data Sheet 1067EN (2018) conservative screening

title: Ferritic SS — limited sour application
notes: Ferritic SS restricted to mild-sour due to 475 °C embrittlement + Cl-pitting susceptibility. Per ATI/AK Steel 430 + 444 product data, conservative-screening bounds.
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.7 + ATI 430 Tech Data (2018) + AK Steel 444 Product Data (2017)

title: Highly-alloyed austenitic — downhole / OCTG service

envelope.needs_review: Downhole sub-row of A.4/A.5 family; Annex B qualification typically required

ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.8 + NACE Corrosion 2018 Paper 11250 (Smith) on super-austenitic OCTG

title: Highly-alloyed austenitic — restricted-mass internals

envelope.needs_review: Higher T allowed for low-mass internals per A.3-style reasoning; project verification required

ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.9 conservative screening

title: Highly-alloyed austenitic — Alloy 825 / Sanicro 28
notes: Alloy 825 + Sanicro 28 OCTG/downhole envelope per Special Metals Incoloy 825 Brochure + Sandvik Sanicro 28 Technical Data; widely cited in NACE Corrosion 2007 Paper 07091 (Rhodes).
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.10 + Special Metals Incoloy 825 Pub.SMC-029 (2013) + Sandvik Sanicro 28 (2019) + Rhodes P.R. (2007) Corrosion 63, 63

title: Highly-alloyed austenitic — Alloy 20 / 28 alternate

envelope.needs_review: Alternate sub-envelope per ISO 15156-3 A.11; public vendor sources cover A.10 only

ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.11 conservative screening

title: Stabilised ferritic SS — alternate equipment (444 / SeaCure / AL-29-4C)
notes: Nb/Ti-stabilised ferritic SS resist sensitization and have moderately better Cl-SCC than 430. Per ATI AL-29-4C + Plymouth Tube SeaCure datasheets.
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.12 + ATI AL-29-4C Tech Data (2018) + Plymouth Tube SeaCure Brochure (2019)

title: Solid-solution Ni-Cr-Mo for downhole / production tubing (G3 / G30)
notes: Per Haynes G-3 and G-30 product brochures: 'any combination' acceptable when annealed, HRC ≤ 22.
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.13 + Haynes G-3 Brochure H-2000B (2016) + Haynes G-30 Brochure H-2024 (2017)

title: Solid-solution Ni-Cr-Mo for general equipment — any combination envelope
notes: Any combination of T/Cl/pH2S/pH when annealed and HRC ≤ 22. C-276, C-22, 59, C-2000 = the gold-standard sour-CRA family.
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.14 + Haynes C-276 Brochure H-2002F (2017) + Haynes C-22 Brochure H-2019 + ASM v.13B (2005) Ni-Cr-Mo chapter

title: Highly-alloyed austenitic — high-Cl pressure equipment
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.15 conservative screening

title: Solid-solution Ni alloys — sub-envelope 16 (annealed pressure-containing)

envelope.needs_review: Alternate sub-row per A.16; A.33/A.34 'any combination' verdict preferred when alloy qualifies for them

notes: Alternate sub-row for very-high-T+H2S service per Haynes / Special Metals technical bulletins on Alloy 625 / C-276 in HPHT sour applications. A.33 / A.34 (any-combination) preferred verdict for these UNS when applicable.
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.16 + Special Metals Inconel 625 Pub.SMC-063 (2013) + NACE Corrosion 2014 Paper 4140 (Rhodes) HPHT sour CRA selection

title: Solid-solution Ni alloys — cold-worked alternate

envelope.needs_review: Cold-worked sub-envelope per A.17; project verification required

ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.17 conservative screening

title: Martensitic SS — general equipment (13Cr / S13Cr)
notes: S41000 / S41425 / cast J91540 sour-service general equipment per API 6A martensitic CRA spec + Sumitomo Super-13Cr datasheet; HRC 22 cap + double-tempering.
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.18 + API 6A (21st ed., 2018) Annex 6A CRA Spec + Sumitomo Super-13Cr Brochure (2016)

title: Martensitic SS — downhole tubular (13Cr OCTG)
notes: S41425 Q+T 13Cr OCTG — T+H2S+pH are the binding constraints; Cl tolerance is implicit when T+H2S are within envelope (per Marchebois 2003 + Linne 2000 OCTG service data). Sub-row variation by S41425 / S13Cr-110 grade.
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.19 + Marchebois H. et al. (2003) Corrosion 59, 1063 + Linne C.P. (2000) NACE Corrosion 2000 Paper 00136

title: Super-13Cr martensitic (S13Cr-110 / Sumitomo SM13CRS)
notes: Super-13Cr modified with Ni+Mo for enhanced sour tolerance. Per Sumitomo SM13CRS-110 datasheet.
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.20 + Sumitomo SM13CRS-110 Tech Bulletin (2017) + Linne C.P. (2000) NACE Corrosion 2000 Paper 00136

title: Modified 15Cr martensitic (HP-15Cr / Nippon Steel)

envelope.needs_review: Nippon Steel HP-15Cr datasheet gives general envelope; project verification required

ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.21 + Nippon Steel HP-15Cr Brochure (2018) conservative screening

title: S13Cr-110 alternate sub-envelope

envelope.needs_review: Sub-envelope per A.22; alternate test condition

ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.22 conservative screening

title: Martensitic SS — additional sub-envelope (downhole)
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.23 conservative screening

title: Duplex SS 22Cr — general equipment
notes: 22Cr lean-duplex envelope pH2S ≤ 10 kPa
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.24 (22Cr sub-row) + Technical Circular 1 (2021)

title: Super-duplex SS 25Cr — general equipment
notes: 25Cr super-duplex envelope per 2020 edition
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.24 (25Cr sub-row) + Technical Circular 1 (2021)

title: Duplex SS — downhole / pressure-containing alternate
notes: Tighter pH2S + lower HRC for downhole / pressure-containing service vs A.24. Per Sandvik SAF 2205 + SAF 2507 downhole bulletins.
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.25 + Sandvik SAF 2205 Brochure S-1841-ENG (2018) + SAF 2507 Brochure S-027-ENG (2017)

title: Precipitation-hardened austenitic SS (A286)
notes: A286 (PH austenitic) for high-T bolting in sour service. Per Special Metals A286 datasheet + ASTM A453 Gr.660 mechanical properties.
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.26 + Special Metals A286 Pub.SMC-001 (2014) + ASTM A453 (2017)

title: Precipitation-hardened martensitic SS (17-4PH / 15-5PH)
notes: 17-4PH / 15-5PH H1150 double-aged + 50 % SMYS sustained-stress limit per ISO 15156-3 (2020) Technical Circular 1 restriction (S17400 cracking incidents reported).
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.27 + Technical Circular 1 (2021) S17400 stress restriction + Carpenter Custom 450 + AK Steel 17-4PH Tech Data (2018)

title: Solid-solution Ni-Fe-Cr alternate (Alloy 31)

envelope.needs_review: Alternate per A.28; vendor literature focuses on Alloy 31 in phosphoric/sulphuric service

ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.28 + VDM Alloy 31 Material Data Sheet 8023 (2017)

title: Solid-solution Ni-Cr-Mo alternate (G-3 / G-30 alternate)

envelope.needs_review: Alternate per A.29; conservative bound from Haynes G-30 datasheet

ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.29 + Haynes G-30 Brochure H-2024 (2017) conservative screening

title: Alloy 825 alternate envelope (cold-worked)

envelope.needs_review: Cold-worked sub-row for tubing per A.30; Special Metals 825 cold-worked range

ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.30 + Special Metals Incoloy 825 cold-worked tubing data (2014) conservative screening

title: Alloy 59 envelope (VDM Alloy 59)
notes: Alloy 59 (UNS N06059) — highest-Mo Ni-Cr-Mo (~16 % Mo) — any combination of T/Cl/pH2S/pH when annealed per VDM datasheet, comparable to A.14 framework.
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.31 + VDM Alloy 59 Material Data Sheet 5022 (2018)

title: Precipitation-hardened nickel-based alloys (restructured 2020)
notes: PH Ni alloys (Alloy 718, 725, 925, 945 etc.) per API 6A CRA spec with mandatory two-step age (cited as Annex B compliant). Multiple sub-rows by UNS — Alloy 718 is the benchmark.
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.32 (restructured) + Technical Circular 1 (2021) + API 6A (21st ed., 2018) Annex 6A + Special Metals Inconel 718 Pub.SMC-045 (2013)

title: Solid-solution Ni-Cr-Mo — Alloy 625 production tubing
notes: Any combination of T/Cl/pH2S/pH when annealed (≤22 HRC).

title: Solid-solution Ni — Alloy 625 / C-276 / 22 valves general
notes: Any combination when annealed.

title: Solid-solution Ni — alternate (cold-worked tubing)
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.35 + Special Metals Inconel 625 cold-worked tubing (2014) conservative screening

title: Solid-solution — Alloy 825 alternate (HPHT)
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.36 + Rhodes P.R. (2007) Corrosion 63, 63 conservative screening

title: Solution-annealed Ni — alternate envelope 37
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.37 conservative screening

title: Solution-annealed Ni — alternate envelope 38
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.38 conservative screening

title: Solution-annealed Ni — Alloy 625 HPHT alternate

envelope.needs_review: HPHT cold-worked envelope per Smith 1994 Corrosion 50, 156

ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.39 + Smith L.M. et al. (1994) Corrosion 50, 156 — Inconel 625 cold-worked in sour HPHT to 232 °C

title: Cobalt-based alloys — diaphragms, pressure devices, seals, springs (Elgiloy / MP35N / Co-alloy)
notes: Co-base alloys (Elgiloy R30003 / MP35N R30035 / MP159 R30159) for diaphragms/springs — any combination acceptable in cold-drawn + aged spring temper.
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.40 + Elgiloy Specialty Metals Datasheet (2019) + Special Metals MP35N Pub.SMC-046 (2014)

title: Titanium and titanium alloys (CP Gr 2/4 / Gr 7 / Gr 12 / Gr 29 / Gr 28)
notes: Ti alloys immune to SSC in sour service below ~250 °C α-corrosion limit. R55400 (Gr 28 α-β with Pd+Ru) added in 2020 edition. R53400 (Gr 12) must be annealed only.
ref: ISO 15156-3:2020 Table A.41 + Technical Circular 1 (2021) + Schutz R.W. & Thomas D.E. (1987) ASM Handbook v.13 Ti + TIMET TICORR Data Sheet (2017)
